questions.q: In which century did the mathematician Bhaskara II live and work?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II was a 12th-century mathematician and astronomer, born in 1114 CE.

questions.q: By what honorific title is Bhaskara II also widely known?
questions.explanation: He is commonly called Bhaskaracharya, meaning 'Bhaskara the teacher'.

questions.q: What was Bhaskara II's primary field of expertise?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II was renowned as both a mathematician and an astronomer.

questions.q: Which famous mathematical treatise by Bhaskara II is often named after a woman?
questions.explanation: Lilavati is Bhaskara II's celebrated treatise on arithmetic and algebra.

questions.q: Bhaskara II's major work Siddhanta Shiromani belongs to which broad subject?
questions.explanation: The Siddhanta Shiromani is a comprehensive treatise on mathematics and astronomy.

questions.q: Anticipating ideas of which branch of mathematics is Bhaskara II especially credited with?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II anticipated several ideas of calculus, including the derivative, centuries before Europe.

questions.q: In which present-day country did Bhaskara II live and work?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II was an Indian mathematician and astronomer.

questions.q: Bhaskara II built on the work of which earlier Indian mathematician famous for rules involving zero?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II extended the algebraic tradition of Brahmagupta, who set out rules for zero and negative numbers.

questions.q: Lilavati is best known as a treatise on which subject?
questions.explanation: Lilavati deals primarily with arithmetic, mensuration, and elementary algebra.

questions.q: Roughly how many centuries before European calculus did Bhaskara II explore related ideas?
options: At the same time as Newton
questions.explanation: Working in the 12th century, Bhaskara II preceded Newton and Leibniz's calculus by roughly five centuries.

questions.q: In which year is Bhaskara II generally said to have been born?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II was born in 1114 CE, as he himself noted in his writings.

questions.q: The Siddhanta Shiromani is traditionally divided into how many main parts?
questions.explanation: The Siddhanta Shiromani has four parts: Lilavati, Bijaganita, Grahaganita, and Goladhyaya.

questions.q: Which part of the Siddhanta Shiromani is devoted to algebra?
questions.explanation: Bijaganita is the algebra section of the Siddhanta Shiromani.

questions.q: Which section of the Siddhanta Shiromani deals with the sphere and spherical astronomy?
questions.explanation: Goladhyaya is the part of the Siddhanta Shiromani concerning the celestial sphere.

questions.q: Bhaskara II is associated with leading the observatory at which famous center of astronomy?
questions.explanation: He is traditionally linked to the astronomical observatory at Ujjain, a major center of Indian astronomy.

questions.q: Which infinitesimal idea, central to calculus, did Bhaskara II employ in his astronomical work?
questions.explanation: He used a concept akin to the instantaneous rate of change (the derivative) when computing planetary motion.

questions.q: Bhaskara II stated a result closely related to which idea later known in calculus?
questions.explanation: His treatment of planetary positions reflects a form of the mean value / Rolle's theorem idea.

questions.q: In Lilavati, Bhaskara II discusses operations involving which special number whose division he addressed?
options: The square root of negative one
questions.explanation: Lilavati and Bijaganita discuss arithmetic with zero, including division involving zero.

questions.q: Bhaskara II's father, who was also his teacher, was named:
questions.explanation: His father Mahesvara (Maheshwara) was an astrologer-astronomer who taught him mathematics.

questions.q: Which of these number-theory problems did Bhaskara II advance the solution of?
questions.explanation: He refined the chakravala (cyclic) method for solving Pell's equation, an indeterminate quadratic equation.

questions.q: The cyclic algorithm Bhaskara II used and refined for indeterminate equations is called:
questions.explanation: The chakravala (cyclic) method solves indeterminate quadratic equations such as Pell's equation.

questions.q: Approximately when did Bhaskara II die?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II is generally believed to have died around 1185 CE.

questions.q: Bhaskara II composed which shorter astronomical handbook intended as a practical almanac-style text?
questions.explanation: Karanakutuhala (c. 1183) is Bhaskara II's practical astronomical handbook for computing positions.

questions.q: In Bijaganita, Bhaskara II discussed which counterintuitive quantity that he sometimes called 'khahara'?
questions.explanation: He treated a finite quantity divided by zero as an unbounded ('khahara', infinite) quantity.

questions.q: Bhaskara II gave an early statement of which differential result for the sine function?
options: The integral of sine equals sine
The derivative of sine relates to the cosine
Sine has no rate of change
The sine equals its own second derivative
questions.explanation: He expressed the instantaneous change of sine in terms of cosine, an early form of d(sin)/dx = cos.

questions.q: Which name is given to the section of the Siddhanta Shiromani dealing with planetary mathematics and computation?
questions.explanation: Grahaganita is the part covering mathematical (planetary) astronomy and computation of planetary positions.

questions.q: Bhaskara II provided multiple proofs of which classical geometric theorem in his works?
questions.explanation: He gave elegant dissection-based demonstrations of the Pythagorean theorem.

questions.q: Which astronomical idea did Bhaskara II uphold, consistent with the earlier Indian tradition of Aryabhata?
options: A roughly accurate length for the sidereal year
That comets are made of ice
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II computed a sidereal year close to the modern value, building on Aryabhata's astronomy.

questions.q: What does the term 'Bijaganita', the title of his algebra text, literally refer to?
options: Seed-counting, i.e., the analysis of unknowns ('seeds')
questions.explanation: Bijaganita means 'mathematics of seeds (bija)', treating unknown quantities as algebraic seeds.

questions.q: Bhaskara II's work shows an understanding that a positive number has how many square roots?
options: Two, one positive and one negative
questions.explanation: He recognized that a positive number has two square roots, one positive and one negative.
